title: DSDS Component Definitions
description: Defines the component document. A component document holds the identity of a reusable UI element. The `metadata` object holds the shared entityMetadata fields (see metadata/metadata.schema.json). The `documentBlocks` array holds structured docs as typed entries. It accepts the component-scoped kinds (imports, anatomy, api, variants, states, design-specifications) plus the general kinds (guidelines, use-cases, accessibility, content, sections, checklist).

component.description: A single reusable UI component. The top level holds its identity: `identifier`, `name`, and `description`. The `metadata` object holds the shared entityMetadata fields (see metadata/metadata.schema.json). The `documentBlocks` array holds structured docs as typed entries: imports, anatomy, api, variants, states, design-specifications, plus the general kinds.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a component.

identifier.description: Machine-readable identifier (e.g., 'button', 'dialog', 'form-field', 'data-table'). MUST be unique within its entity group.

name.description: Display name shown in docs (e.g., 'Button', 'Dialog', 'Form Field', 'Data Table').

description.description: What this component is, what it does, and its role in the design system. CommonMark supported.

metadata.description: Optional metadata: the shared entityMetadata fields (see metadata/metadata.schema.json).

relationships.description: Typed dependency edges from this component to other entities — e.g. 'depends-on' a token, 'composes' another component, 'alternative-to' a sibling. The structural graph agents read for impact and alternatives. Inverse edges are derived by tooling. Use the `links` metadata field for external resources.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this component, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. It accepts the component-specific kinds (imports, anatomy, api, variants, states, design-specifications) plus the general kinds (guidelines, use-cases, accessibility, content, sections, checklist). The `kind` property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters, so tools SHOULD keep it for display.

agentDocumentBlocks.description: Structured docs for agents (AI/LLM) only — a separate space that accepts the same block kinds as `documentBlocks`. `documentBlocks` is the default: put anything a human reader needs there. Use `agentDocumentBlocks` only for content that helps an agent build correctly but is noise for humans. For example: hard MUST/MUST-NOT rules an agent should treat as firm limits; notes that correct a mistake an agent is likely to make, such as reaching for this entity when a confusable one fits better; constraints backed by test-run evidence; and machine-checkable criteria with verification modes. A hard rule that humans also need (such as an accessibility requirement) belongs in `documentBlocks`, not here. Agent blocks add to the human docs — they never replace or duplicate them. Tools MUST NOT render these blocks in human-facing docs. Agents SHOULD read both arrays, human blocks first.
